source_name: CDC Key Points about Developmental Milestone Checklists
key_takeaway: CDC milestones are for monitoring/surveillance, not diagnosis or screening; missed milestones should prompt discussion or further action.
notes: Use as core safety language.

source_name: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. About
key_takeaway: CDC supports family-engaged developmental monitoring and encourages families to talk with providers and ask about screening when concerned.
notes: Good model for tone: celebrate, monitor, act early.

source_name: Evidence-Informed Milestones for Developmental Surveillance Tools
key_takeaway: CDC revised milestones use an evidence-informed process, observable milestones, plain language, and a threshold where most children, 75% or more, are expected to demonstrate the milestone.
notes: Use to justify why CDC age placement is used.

source_name: Promoting Optimal Development: Developmental Surveillance and Screening
primary_product_use: Distinguish surveillance vs screening vs evaluation
key_takeaway: AAP recommends ongoing surveillance and standardized developmental screening at target ages and whenever concerns arise.
notes: Supports professional referral logic.

source_name: Bright Futures Guidelines and Pocket Guide
key_takeaway: Bright Futures provides age-based preventive care and anticipatory guidance structure for pediatric visits.
notes: Use for visit schedule and clinician-friendly language.

source_name: WHO Motor Development Milestones
key_takeaway: WHO provides windows of achievement for six gross motor milestones from the Multicentre Growth Reference Study.
notes: Use for motor nuance beyond single age points.

source_name: Nurturing Care Framework for Early Childhood Development
primary_product_use: ECD framing, responsive caregiving, early learning
key_takeaway: Children need health, nutrition, security and safety, responsive caregiving and opportunities for early learning.
notes: Use for holistic EU-friendly framing.

source_name: Framework on Early Childhood Development in the WHO European Region
key_takeaway: European ECD framework highlights young children's needs, monitoring development, responding to concerns, and social/environmental risks.
notes: Core EU positioning source.

source_name: Serve and Return
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
key_takeaway: Responsive back-and-forth interactions shape brain architecture and support social, emotional and cognitive skills.
notes: Use for language/social-emotional activity explanations.

source_name: A Guide to Serve & Return
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
key_takeaway: Caregiver responsiveness to a child's signals creates serve-and-return experiences that support development.
notes: Useful for parent-friendly wording.

source_name: How-to: 5 Steps for Brain-Building Serve and Return
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
primary_product_use: Concrete parent-facing steps for responsive interaction
key_takeaway: Parents can notice a child's signal, return it, name it, take turns, and practice endings/beginnings in everyday routines.
notes: Use for short interaction cards and scripts.

source_name: Brain-Building Through Play: Activities for Infants, Toddlers, and Children
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child / LEGO Foundation
primary_product_use: Age-based play ideas and executive-function practice
key_takeaway: Simple playful interactions help children practice attention, working memory and self-control while strengthening relationships.
notes: Use as activity-form source, not as milestone proof.

source_name: A Guide to Executive Function
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
key_takeaway: Executive-function skills develop through supportive environments and practice, including attention, working memory, flexibility and self-control.
notes: Useful for toddler+ and future behavior/routine products.

source_name: Activities Guide: Enhancing and Practicing Executive Function Skills with Children from Infancy to Adolescence
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
key_takeaway: Age-appropriate games and routines can help children practice executive function from infancy onward.
notes: Use for future self-regulation/routine cards.

source_name: Play in Early Childhood: The Role of Play in Any Setting
organization: Harvard Center on the Developing Child
primary_product_use: Play as mechanism for relationships, core skills and stress reduction
key_takeaway: Play supports responsive relationships, core life skills and stress buffering in early childhood.
notes: Use for play-based recommendation rationale.

source_name: CDC Milestones by 2 Months
key_takeaway: Parent-facing milestones and activity tips for 2 months. CDC states these materials are not substitutes for standardized validated developmental screening tools.
notes: Age-specific source ID for MVP map.

source_name: CDC Milestones by 4 Months
key_takeaway: Parent-facing milestones and activity tips for 4 months. Use as source for observable milestone structure.
notes: Age-specific source ID for MVP map.

source_name: CDC Milestones by 6 Months
key_takeaway: Parent-facing milestones and activity tips for 6 months. Use as source for 6-month review-ready page.
notes: Age-specific source ID for MVP map and sample page.

source_name: CDC Milestones by 9 Months
key_takeaway: Parent-facing milestones and activity tips for 9 months. Use as source for 9-month MVP map.
notes: Age-specific source ID for MVP map.

source_name: CDC Milestones by 1 Year
key_takeaway: Parent-facing milestones and activity tips for 1 year. Use as source for 12-month MVP map.
notes: Age-specific source ID for MVP map.
